calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-10-2019 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-16-2018 Set For Trial On Jan-14-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Atain Specialty Insurance Company For Leave To Intervene
Off Calendar Dec-12-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Andrew McCleland of the law offices of Lunski & Stimpert (415-426-3521) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Shaun W. Joyce of the law offices of Stone & Associate (925-938-1555) appears by telephone as counsel for intervenor/moving party, Atain Specialty Insurance Company

No other appearance is made in this matter

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Court reviews a case cited by counsel, "Pulte".

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

INTERVENOR ATAIN SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion For Leave To Intervene is granted. Attain has leave to file its proposed complaint in intervention no later than December 22, 2017. Per Clemmer v. Hartford Insurance Co. (1978) 22 Cal. 3d 865, 886 and subsequent insurer intervention case law, Attain is entitled to intervene in this case to protect its own interests. Atain has shown that it has an interest as the insurer of defendant Bertram Harris which warrants intervention, it has followed the proper procedures for intervention, and it has acted with reasonable diligence. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR No. 11185, e-mail: adelgado@sftc.org (L&M302)

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Dec-12-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Andrew McCleland of the law offices of Lunski & Stimpert (415-426-3521) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Shaun W. Joyce of the law offices of Stone & Associate (925-938-1555) appears by telephone as counsel for intervenor/moving party, Atain Specialty Insurance Company

No other appearance is made in this matter

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. Court reviews a case cited by counsel, "Pulte".

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

INTERVENOR ATAIN SPECIALTY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is granted. Attain has leave to file its proposed complaint in intervention no later than December 22, 2017. Per Clemmer v. Hartford Insurance Co. (1978) 22 Cal. 3d 865, 886 and subsequent insurer intervention case law, Attain is entitled to intervene in this case to protect its own interests. Atain has shown that it has an interest as the insurer of defendant Bertram Harris which warrants intervention, it has followed the proper procedures for intervention, and it has acted with reasonable diligence. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR No. 11185, e-mail: adelgado@sftc.org (L&M302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-19-2017 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-16-2017 Continued To Aug-09-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Plaintiffs Request For Default Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-22-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: J. Bertelsen; Reporter: Mitch Lyon, CSR#6840.

Ron Lunski, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff, Bethany Brodsky, also present.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Bertram Harris dba Redesign2Renovate.
Default of Defendant has been duly entered.
Witness Bethany Brodsky and Jake Millan sworn and examined on behalf of Plaintiff.
After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant as follows:

Damages: $50,244.80
Pre-Judgment Interest: $0
Attorney's Fees: $7,000.00
Costs: $450.00
Total Judgment in the amount of $57,694.80
Judgment signed in open court.
